question: A developer is building a new AI solution on Azure that requires generative AI chat capabilities, speech-to-text, and document intelligence features. What is the recommended approach for organizing these resources in Microsoft Foundry?

options.text: Create a separate Azure resource for each AI tool (Speech, Document Intelligence, etc.) and connect them individually.
Create a Microsoft Foundry resource that hosts the project, and access all Foundry Tools through the project endpoint.
Provision an Azure Machine Learning workspace and install AI toolkits as packages.
Deploy all AI capabilities inside a single Azure Container App with custom AI libraries.

explanation: Microsoft Foundry provides a single platform for AI development where Foundry Tools (including Azure Language, Speech, Document Intelligence, etc.) are hosted in a Foundry resource. Developers access all tools through the project endpoint, making it the recommended approach for new projects rather than provisioning isolated individual resources.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Plan and prepare to develop AI solutions on Azure

question: You need to authenticate calls to an Azure Translator Foundry resource using a multi-service (Foundry) resource key. Which additional HTTP header must you include when using the Foundry resource key specifically with Azure Translator?
explanation: When using a Foundry (multi-service) resource key with Azure Translator, you must include the Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Region header to specify the resource region. This is a Translator-specific requirement when using the multi-service key; single-service keys for Translator do not require this header.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: Complete the following sentences about Foundry Tools authentication by selecting the correct value for each blank.

blanks.prompt: To authenticate a request using a resource key for a specific Foundry Tool, the key is passed in the _____ HTTP header.
An authentication token obtained from the STS token exchange endpoint is valid for _____ before expiring.
Microsoft Entra ID authentication for Foundry Tools requires the resource to use a _____ rather than a regional endpoint.

explanation: Foundry Tools authentication: resource keys are passed as Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key; access tokens from the STS endpoint are valid for exactly 10 minutes and stored in JWT format; Microsoft Entra ID authentication always requires a custom subdomain name—regional endpoints do not support Entra auth.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: An Azure Function App needs to call the Azure Vision API (a Foundry Tool) without storing credentials in application settings. The organization mandates the most secure approach. What should the developer configure?

options.text: Store the API key as an environment variable in the Function App's application settings.
Hard-code the API key in the function source code and redeploy when rotating keys.
Enable a managed identity on the Function App and assign the Cognitive Services User role to it on the Foundry resource.
Generate an access token by calling the STS endpoint every 10 minutes from a separate timer function.

explanation: The most secure authentication method for Azure workloads calling Foundry Tools is to use managed identity with Microsoft Entra ID. Enabling a managed identity on the Function App and assigning it the Cognitive Services User role on the Foundry resource avoids storing any credentials in the application, and is the approach recommended in the Foundry Tools authentication documentation.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: Complete the following sentences about Foundry Tools security features by selecting the correct value for each blank.

blanks.prompt: All Foundry Tools endpoints exposed over HTTP enforce a minimum TLS version of _____ to protect data in transit.
The Foundry security feature that prevents a malicious insider from exfiltrating sensitive company data by submitting sensitive URLs as API inputs is called _____.
Each Foundry resource has _____ API keys to enable secret rotation without service disruption.

explanation: Foundry Tools security: all endpoints enforce TLS 1.2 (optionally 1.3) for data in transit. The Data loss prevention (DLP) feature prevents insiders from submitting sensitive company URLs to Azure AI services. Each Foundry resource has two API keys specifically to support zero-downtime key rotation—switching between them avoids any disruption during a rotation.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: Your company stores sensitive customer data in an Azure Language (Foundry Tool) resource for longer than 48 hours. The security team requires an additional encryption layer beyond the default Azure encryption, using keys that the company controls. Which Foundry Tools security feature should you configure?

options.text: Customer-managed keys (CMK) linked to Azure Key Vault

explanation: Customer-managed keys (CMK) are designed for Foundry Tools services that store customer data at rest for longer than 48 hours. By linking the service to Azure Key Vault and managing the encryption keys, organizations add an extra layer of encryption on top of the default Azure server-side encryption, giving them full control over the keys.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: You need to restrict which network endpoints can make API calls to a Foundry Tools resource so that only resources within your corporate virtual network can reach it. Which Foundry Tools security feature addresses this requirement?

options.text: Virtual networks (network rules) for the Foundry resource
Access token authentication via the STS endpoint

explanation: Virtual network rules for Foundry Tools allow administrators to specify which network endpoints (IP ranges or virtual network subnets) can make API calls to the resource. The Azure service will reject calls from devices outside the configured network, providing network-level isolation for AI workloads.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: When creating a private endpoint for a Microsoft Foundry resource in the Azure portal, what region constraint must the private endpoint satisfy?

options.text: The private endpoint must be in a different region from the Foundry resource to enable geo-redundancy.
The private endpoint must be deployed in the same region as the virtual network it connects to.
The private endpoint can be in any region as long as it is in the same subscription.
The private endpoint must be in the East US or West US region regardless of the virtual network location.

explanation: According to the Microsoft Foundry private link documentation, the private endpoint must be deployed in the same region as the virtual network. This is a hard requirement when configuring inbound network isolation for a Foundry resource using a private endpoint.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation
source.unit: How to configure network isolation for Microsoft Foundry

question: After adding a private endpoint to a Microsoft Foundry project (with public access disabled), a developer on the corporate VPN reports they still cannot connect. From inside the virtual network, running nslookup on the Foundry endpoint hostname returns the public IP. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The private endpoint connection is still in Pending state and has not been approved.
A private DNS zone for the privatelink subdomain is missing or not linked to the virtual network.
The Foundry resource TLS version is set to 1.2 instead of 1.3.
The developer's service principal does not have the Cognitive Services User role.

explanation: When nslookup from inside the virtual network resolves to the public IP instead of the private endpoint IP, it indicates a DNS configuration problem. The private DNS zone for the privatelink subdomain must exist and be linked to the virtual network. Without this, clients inside the VNet still receive the public DNS record rather than resolving to the private endpoint IP address.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation
source.unit: How to configure network isolation for Microsoft Foundry

question: An enterprise is deploying a network-isolated Microsoft Foundry resource with virtual network injection for their AI Agent Service. Which set of BYO (Bring Your Own) resources is required to enable Standard Agent deployment with end-to-end VNet isolation?

options.text: Azure Blob Storage and Azure Key Vault
Azure Storage, Azure AI Search, and Azure Cosmos DB
Azure Service Bus, Azure Event Hub, and Azure Monitor
Azure Container Registry and Azure Kubernetes Service

explanation: When setting up Foundry with virtual network injection for end-to-end network isolation, the Standard Agent setup requires you to bring your own Azure Storage, Azure AI Search, and Azure Cosmos DB resources. All data processed by the Foundry Agent Service is stored at rest in these BYO resources, helping meet compliance and enterprise security requirements.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation
source.unit: How to configure network isolation for Microsoft Foundry

question: Complete the following sentences about Microsoft Foundry's network isolation features by selecting the correct value for each blank.

blanks.prompt: When a private endpoint is created for a Foundry resource, Azure updates the DNS CNAME record to an alias in a subdomain with the prefix _____.
For virtual network injection with the Foundry Agent Service, the delegated subnet (delegated to Microsoft.App/environments) must be _____ or larger.
In the Foundry private networking model with virtual network injection, the IP address range _____ must NOT be used for the virtual network as it is reserved by Docker bridge networking.

explanation: Foundry network isolation key facts: when a private endpoint is created, Azure updates the CNAME to an alias in the 'privatelink' subdomain and auto-creates a private DNS zone; the Agent Service VNet injection subnet delegated to Microsoft.App/environments must be /27 or larger; and the 172.17.0.0/16 range is reserved by Docker bridge networking and must not be used for the VNet.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation
source.unit: How to configure network isolation for Microsoft Foundry

question: A developer is building generative AI applications in Visual Studio Code and wants to browse deployed models, test agents in integrated playgrounds, and generate integration code directly from the IDE. Which tool should they install?

options.text: Azure CLI extension for VS Code
The Foundry Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code
The Azure Machine Learning VS Code extension

explanation: The Foundry Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code is specifically designed for Microsoft Foundry-based generative AI development. It enables browsing project resources, deploying models, testing in integrated playgrounds, configuring agents, and generating integration code—all directly within VS Code.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Plan and prepare to develop AI solutions on Azure

question: Microsoft's Responsible AI guidelines define six core principles for AI development. A product team realizes their AI model generates loan denial recommendations that are systematically less favorable for applicants from a specific ethnic group. Which responsible AI principle is being violated?
explanation: The Fairness principle requires that AI systems treat all people equitably, without bias based on gender, ethnicity, or other factors that may create an unfair advantage or disadvantage for specific groups. A model that produces systematically less favorable outcomes for a specific ethnic group violates the Fairness principle.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Plan and prepare to develop AI solutions on Azure

question: Your organization's AI chatbot occasionally generates factually incorrect medical information. Users are not informed that outputs may be inaccurate, nor told what data the model was trained on. Which responsible AI principle is most directly violated?
explanation: The Transparency principle requires that AI systems be understandable: users should be made fully aware of the purpose of the system, how it works, and what limitations may be expected. Not informing users about potential inaccuracies or the factors affecting predictions directly violates this principle.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Plan and prepare to develop AI solutions on Azure

question: When planning a responsible generative AI solution using the Microsoft four-stage framework, what is the correct order of the stages?
explanation: Microsoft's responsible generative AI framework defines four sequential stages: (1) Map potential harms, (2) Measure the presence of those harms, (3) Mitigate the harms, and (4) Manage the solution responsibly. These stages correspond closely to the functions in the NIST AI Risk Management Framework.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry
source.unit: Plan a responsible generative AI solution

question: During the Map stage of responsible AI planning for a generative AI cooking assistant, your team has produced a prioritized list of potential harms. What is the NEXT step within the Map stage after prioritizing harms?

options.text: Implement content filters in the safety system layer.
Test and verify the prioritized harms occur and under what conditions.
Create an incident response plan for when harms are discovered post-release.
Deploy the solution to a restricted group of beta users to gather real-world data.

explanation: Within the Map stage, after identifying and prioritizing potential harms, the next step is to test and verify that the prioritized harms actually occur (and under what conditions). This may involve red team testing and can reveal previously unidentified harms to add to the list.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: A security engineer is conducting red team testing on a generative AI application. What is the primary purpose of red teaming in the context of responsible AI for generative AI solutions?

options.text: To evaluate the response latency of the model under high load conditions.
To deliberately probe the solution for weaknesses and attempt to produce harmful outputs.
To validate the accuracy of factual responses against a ground-truth dataset.
To ensure that the model meets GDPR data residency requirements.

explanation: Red teaming for generative AI solutions involves having a team of testers deliberately probe the solution for weaknesses and attempt to produce harmful results. It is used during the Map/verification step to confirm that potential harms occur under certain conditions, building on existing cybersecurity red-teaming practices extended to content safety.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: When measuring potential harms in a generative AI solution, what is the recommended testing approach to achieve coverage at scale after initial validation?

options.text: Rely entirely on user feedback submitted through the production application.
Start with manual testing of a small prompt set, then automate testing with a larger volume of test cases, including a classification model to evaluate output.
Only run automated testing pipelines without manual review to eliminate human bias.
Perform a single comprehensive manual test before launch and skip further testing after deployment.

explanation: The Measure unit recommends starting with manual testing to validate consistency and evaluation criteria, then automating testing with a larger volume of test cases—potentially using a classification model to evaluate output. Even after automation is implemented, periodic manual testing should continue to validate new scenarios.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: Microsoft Foundry's content filter guardrails classify content into severity levels for five categories. Which of the following is one of the five harm categories used by Foundry guardrails content filters?
explanation: Microsoft Foundry's guardrails apply content filters that classify content into four severity levels (safe, low, medium, high) across five harm categories: hate and fairness, sexual, violence, self-harm, and task-adherence. Self-harm is one of the five named categories explicitly used by the safety system layer.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: At which mitigation layer in the responsible generative AI framework would you apply Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to reduce the risk of factually inaccurate responses?
explanation: The system message and grounding layer focuses on the construction of prompts submitted to the model. RAG is applied at this layer to retrieve contextual data from trusted data sources and include it in prompts, maximizing the likelihood of relevant, non-harmful, and factually grounded output.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: Before releasing a responsible generative AI solution, which of the following is a key operational capability that should be implemented to handle unanticipated harmful outputs discovered post-launch?

options.text: The ability to immediately block harmful system responses when they are discovered.
A plan to retrain the underlying LLM model within 24 hours of any reported incident.
Mandatory weekly red team exercises with external contractors.
Automatic deletion of all conversation logs to protect user privacy.

explanation: The Manage unit specifies that a responsible deployment plan must include the capability to immediately block harmful system responses when discovered. Additional operational capabilities include blocking specific users or IP addresses in case of misuse, incident response plans, rollback plans, and user feedback mechanisms.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry
source.unit: Manage a responsible generative AI solution

question: When planning a responsible release of a generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry, which TWO actions are recommended before or during the public launch? (Select two.)

options.text: Conduct a phased delivery plan starting with a restricted group of users to gather feedback before wider release.
Immediately grant all users access to maximize feedback volume during the first week.
Complete prerelease compliance reviews including legal, privacy, security, and accessibility teams.
Remove all telemetry collection to avoid storing user data before privacy policies are finalized.

explanation: The Manage unit recommends two key practices: completing prerelease reviews (legal, privacy, security, accessibility) before release, and devising a phased delivery plan that starts with a restricted group of users to gather feedback and identify problems before wider release. Broad immediate access undermines phased delivery; telemetry is encouraged in a privacy-compliant manner.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry
source.unit: Manage a responsible generative AI solution

question: Which TWO Foundry Tools security features can be used together to restrict access to a Foundry resource to only authorized workloads within your corporate network? (Select two.)

options.text: Virtual network rules that limit which network endpoints can call the API
Private endpoints to secure communication over a private link
Customer Lockbox to approve Microsoft engineer access requests

explanation: Virtual network rules allow you to define which network endpoints can make API calls to the Foundry resource, while private endpoints secure the communication channel by routing traffic over a private link. Both are network-isolation security features for Foundry Tools and can be combined. DLP addresses URI-based data exfiltration, and Customer Lockbox controls Microsoft engineer access—neither restricts network access.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: Arrange the following steps in the correct order to configure inbound network isolation for a new Microsoft Foundry resource using a private endpoint in the Azure portal.

items.text: On the Networking tab, set public network access to Disabled.
Configure the Basics tab of the new Foundry resource.
Add a private endpoint, selecting the same region as the virtual network.
On the Review + create tab, review settings and select Create.
Verify DNS resolves to the private endpoint IP from inside the virtual network.

explanation: The Foundry private link documentation prescribes this sequence: configure the Basics tab first, then go to the Networking tab and disable public access, add the private endpoint selecting the correct region/VNet, complete and create the resource, and finally validate DNS resolution from inside the VNet to confirm the private IP is returned.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation
source.unit: How to configure network isolation for Microsoft Foundry

question: A developer needs to authenticate a service principal to Microsoft Entra ID so it can call Foundry Tools using token-based auth. Place the following steps in the correct order.

items.text: Assign the 'Cognitive Services User' role to the service principal scoped to the Foundry resource.
Create the Foundry resource with a custom subdomain name.
Register a Microsoft Entra application and create a service principal.
Obtain a bearer token using the service principal credentials from the Entra token endpoint.
Call the Foundry Tools API using the bearer token in the Authorization header.

explanation: Entra ID authentication for Foundry Tools requires: first creating the resource with a custom subdomain (required for Entra auth), then registering an Entra application and service principal, assigning the Cognitive Services User role, obtaining a bearer token, and finally making API calls with that token in the Authorization header.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: You are implementing the Measure stage of the responsible generative AI process for a customer-facing AI assistant. Place the following steps in the correct order.

items.text: Apply pre-defined criteria to evaluate and categorize the generated output by harm level.
Prepare a diverse set of input prompts likely to elicit each documented potential harm.
Submit the prompts to the system and retrieve the generated outputs.
Document the measurement results and share them with stakeholders.

explanation: The Measure unit defines a three-step generalized process: prepare prompts likely to elicit harms, submit those prompts and retrieve outputs, then apply pre-defined criteria to categorize the output by harm level. Results should then be documented and shared with stakeholders to track the baseline.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: Arrange the four stages of the Microsoft responsible generative AI process in their correct sequential order.

items.text: Mitigate: Apply mitigation techniques at model, safety system, grounding, and UX layers.
Map: Identify, prioritize, test, and document potential harms.
Manage: Define and follow a deployment and operational readiness plan.
Measure: Create a baseline quantifying harmful outputs in given usage scenarios.

explanation: Microsoft's responsible generative AI framework follows the sequence: Map potential harms first, then Measure their presence to establish a baseline, then Mitigate the identified harms across four layers, and finally Manage the solution responsibly through deployment and operational plans.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry
source.unit: Plan a responsible generative AI solution

question: Place the following steps in correct order to implement the Map stage of responsible AI for a new generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry.

items.text: Prioritize identified harms based on likelihood of occurrence and severity of impact.
Identify potential harms by reviewing model documentation and transparency notes.
Document and share the verified harms with project stakeholders.
Test and verify that the prioritized harms occur through red team testing.

explanation: The Map stage follows four sub-steps: first identify potential harms (by reviewing docs, transparency notes, impact assessment guides), then prioritize them by likelihood and impact, then test/verify through red teaming that the harms actually occur, and finally document and share the verified harms with stakeholders.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry

question: Arrange the following actions in the correct order to perform a zero-downtime API key rotation for a Foundry Tools resource that currently uses KEY1 in all client applications.

items.text: Regenerate KEY1 in the Azure portal.
Update all client applications to use KEY2.
Optionally update client applications to use the newly regenerated KEY1.
Verify all client applications are functioning correctly with KEY2.

explanation: Zero-downtime key rotation uses the two-key design: first switch all clients to KEY2 (while KEY1 is still valid), verify they work, then regenerate KEY1 safely (KEY2 is still valid during regeneration), then optionally migrate clients back to the new KEY1. This avoids any period where all clients have an invalid key.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Azure AI services documentation

question: A team is implementing a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry. Match each mitigation action to the correct layer of the four-layer mitigation framework.

blanks.prompt: Fine-tuning the foundational LLM with domain-specific training data to reduce off-topic responses belongs to the _____ layer.
Configuring Foundry guardrail prompt shields to detect systematic abuse of the system prompt belongs to the _____ layer.
Designing the UI to constrain user inputs to specific topics and applying output validation belongs to the _____ layer.

explanation: The four-layer mitigation framework assigns: fine-tuning to the Model layer (adapting the LLM itself), prompt shields and content filters to the Safety system layer (platform-level guardrails in Foundry), and UI input/output constraints to the User experience layer. RAG and system messages belong to the System message and grounding layer.

source.path: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
source.module: Implement a responsible generative AI solution in Microsoft Foundry
